meaning: n.[植]麦卢卡树(一种桃金娘科树,产于澳洲)
examples: The researchers focused on manuka honey from New Zealand.
A better natural substitute would be Manuka honey or molasses but only in very small amounts.
This research may increase the clinical use of manuka honey as doctors are faced with the threat of diminishingly effective antimicrobial options.
This indicates that existing antibiotics may be more effective against drug-resistant infections if used in combination with manuka honey.
The first is that, when properly processed to a medical grade, manuka honey is effective as a treatment for chronic, infected wounds.
